# UC-22.6.30 · Malware Protection — AV Engine Disabled or Out of Date Events

## Description

Flags endpoints reporting disabled protection or stale pattern files for malware control assurance.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes edr and sourcetypes defender:health. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize health vocabulary; (2) auto-remediate via SOAR; (3) weekly compliance export. Indexes required: `index=edr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=defender:health`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Microsoft Defender, CrowdStrike.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `defender:health` or `crowdstrike:sensor`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize health vocabulary; (2) auto-remediate via SOAR; (3) weekly compliance export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edr sourcetype=defender:health earliest=-1d
| where protection_status!="Active" OR signature_age_days>7
| stats latest(protection_status) as status, max(signature_age_days) as sig_age by host
```

Understanding this SPL

**Malware Protection — AV Engine Disabled or Out of Date Events (A.8.7)** — Flags endpoints reporting disabled protection or stale pattern files for malware control assurance.

Documented **Data sources**: `defender:health` or `crowdstrike:sensor`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft Defender, CrowdStrike. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edr; **sourcetype**: defender:health. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edr, sourcetype=defender:health, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where protection_status!="Active" OR signature_age_days>7` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Map

## SPL

```spl
index=edr sourcetype=defender:health earliest=-1d
| where protection_status!="Active" OR signature_age_days>7
| stats latest(protection_status) as status, max(signature_age_days) as sig_age by host
```

## Visualization

Table, Map. Key fields: `host`, `sig_age`, `status`.
